question: Design a CGOA wrapper for a C library that provides a CRC32 checksum function. The C API is: `uint32_t compute_hash(const uint8_t* buffer, size_t length, int* error_code);` It returns the checksum on success; on error it returns 0 and sets error_code to a non-zero value. Build a Go binding: `func ComputeChecksum(data []byte) (uint32, error)`. Convert the Go byte slice to C types, call compute_hash, check the error code, and return the result. Provide the C header, a minimal C implementation, the Go binding, and a small program that calls ComputeChecksum from two goroutines concurrently on different inputs.
answer: Convert data to C pointer via `(*C.uint8_t)(C.CBytes(data))` and get length with `C.size_t(len(data))`. Call `compute_hash`, check if error_code is non-zero and return an error, otherwise return the uint32 checksum. Free the C bytes with `C.free` after the call. Use `sync.WaitGroup` to coordinate two goroutines, each calling ComputeChecksum independently on different byte slices.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  A[Go []byte input] --> B[C.CBytes allocates C memory]
  B --> C[Cast to *C.uint8_t]
  C --> D[Call compute_hash with buffer, length, &error_code]
  D --> E{error_code == 0?}
  E -->|Yes| F[Return uint32 checksum]
  E -->|No| G[Return error]
  B -.-> H[C.free cleans up C memory]
  F --> I[Goroutine prints result]
  G --> I
  I --> J[sync.WaitGroup coordinates completion]
